• , RAG, GraphRAG) that automate complex engineering tasks such as documentation auditing, requirement generation, and technical knowledge retrieval.
• AI & LLM Engineering: Hands-on experience or deep academic exposure to LLM orchestration and application concepts, including RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation), agentic frameworks, context engineering, grounding, evaluation, and cost/latency optimization.
• Construct AI-Ready Data Layers: Develop and maintain the specialized data structures— including knowledge graphs and vector databases—required to provide high-fidelity context to AI applications across siloed engineering systems.
• Develop Evaluation Frameworks: Define and monitor quantitative performance requirements (accuracy, grounding, latency, and cost) to ensure AI tools meet the rigorous safety and reliability standards of vehicle engineering.
• ) and experience with applications Software Fundamentals: Demonstrated experience with version control (Git) and an understanding of how to move a model from a notebook to a stable, reproducible environment.
• Experience with Unstructured Data: Ability to extract, clean, and structure data from technical documents, requirements, or engineering specifications.
• Design & Deploy AI Agents: Partner with senior technical staff to build and orchestrate agentic AI workflows and LLM-powered systems (e.
• Execute AI Trials: Lead rapid, high-velocity technical trials to evaluate the viability of emerging AI techniques.

• In this role, you will play a key part in defining software requirements, designing embedded solutions, and verifying system performance, ensuring our maintenance systems meet the evolving needs of modern aircraft.
• Collaborate with cross-functional teams to meet software engineering requirements.
• You’ll bridge engineering and IT to ensure complex aerospace development ecosystems perform at scale, meet rigorous standards, and align with regulatory and export‑controlled requirements.
• This position is ideal for engineers experienced in modern software development methodologies, debugging, and performance optimization, who thrive in highly technical, cross‑functional environments.

• Collaborate with cross-functional teams to meet software engineering requirements.
• Collaborate with cross-functional teams to meet software engineering requirements You will report directly to our Senior Engineering Manager and you will work out of our Phoenix, AZ location on a Hybrid work schedule.
